articles.title: NETFLIX STARTS USING GENAI IN ITS SHOWS AND FILMS
POLAROID JUST DROPPED THE MOST ICONIC ANTI-AI AD OF THE YEAR
articles.summary: Polaroid has launched a powerful anti-AI campaign to promote its new Flip camera, emphasizing the value of tangible, real-life experiences over digital saturation. The campaign uses nostalgic Polaroid imagery paired with provocative slogans like “AI can't generate sand between your toes” to highlight the emotional depth and authenticity of analog living. Strategically placed near tech hubs like Apple and Google offices, the campaign has resonated widely online, drawing praise for its aesthetic and timely critique of tech culture.

articles.title: FIGMA ANNOUNCES LAUNCH OF INITIAL PUBLIC OFFERING ROADSHOW
articles.summary: Figma launched its IPO roadshow, offering 12.5 million new shares, plus 24.6 million from existing stockholders, at a price range of $25 to $ 28 per share. The design platform company applied to list on the NYSE under the ticker "FIG" with significant investment banks leading the offering. Figma won't receive proceeds from existing stockholder sales, while underwriters have a 30-day overallotment option for additional shares.

articles.title: AFRAID OF LOSING YOUR DESIGN JOB TO AI? THIS MCDONALD'S STORY MIGHT HELP YOU SLEEP BETTER
articles.summary: Fears that AI will replace creative jobs echo earlier anxieties about McDonald's touchscreen kiosks, which were expected to eliminate human roles but instead led to more jobs and higher sales by enhancing the customer experience. The key takeaway is that technology doesn't determine outcomes—organizations do. Design studios that use AI to amplify human creativity, not just cut costs, will thrive.

articles.title: DESIGNING SOFTWARE FOR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: AI agents increasingly handle computing tasks but struggle with software designed for humans, creating opportunities to redesign systems for machine use. Web search APIs limit results and provide condensed snippets suited for human scanning, while agents could benefit from more comprehensive results and larger document summaries. AgentDB exemplifies agent-first design by utilizing UUIDs for instant database creation, storing databases as files rather than hosting them as services, and integrating with MCP servers to make data easily accessible as AI model context.

articles.title: AI TOOLS + AI FLUENCY + HUMAN ADVANTAGE = AI-NATIVE DESIGNER
articles.summary: Product designers can thrive in the AI era by mastering three key areas: leveraging AI tools for faster shipping, developing AI fluency for designing probabilistic systems, and harnessing human advantages such as craft and creative direction. While AI handles routine tasks and generation, designers must evolve into orchestrators who provide vision, set constraints, and make high-level decisions that guide AI outputs. The future belongs to AI-native designers who combine technical literacy with uniquely human skills like strategic thinking, storytelling, and the ability to create memorable experiences that transcend generic AI outputs.

articles.summary: Find the best UX ideas, trends, and innovations at warp speed.

articles.title: COPY CSS FROM ANY WEBSITE AND ITERATE WITH AI
articles.summary: Design UI with AI, upload a screenshot or describe the idea or UI you want, transform the design to code, and let AI generate the frontend code for you.

articles.title: AI PRESENTATIONS
articles.summary: Workppt can create a compelling starting point for presentations in just minutes. It has everything you need to create and refine content with advanced AI quickly and efficiently.

articles.title: IMPACT STORIES: HOW BLOOM REPOSITIONED GINSTERS (2023)
articles.summary: By 2022, Ginsters faced a perception problem: widely recognized but associated with low quality and outdated imagery, it struggled to attract new customers despite a strong sales history. A strategic rebrand by Bloom in 2023 revitalized the brand through updated design, storytelling, and a more confident tone, resulting in major sales growth, improved brand perception, and successful product innovation aimed at younger audiences.

articles.title: HOW SEMANTICS AND ARIA ATTRIBUTES SUPPORT ACCESSIBLE DESIGN
articles.summary: Accessibility issues stem from missing semantic meaning rather than visual problems, as elements may look correct but fail to communicate their purpose to assistive technologies. Proper semantics using HTML elements, consistent ARIA design patterns, and appropriate attributes work together to create interfaces that screen readers and keyboard users can navigate effectively. Comprehensive accessibility audits must test beyond visual appearance to ensure semantic roles match user expectations and that interactive elements behave predictably for all users.

articles.title: DESIGN TOKENS FOR LINKS
articles.summary: Design systems face an architectural challenge when organizing hyperlinks within token structures, as links occupy a unique position between atomic elements that can exist independently or integrate into complex components. Two approaches emerge: treating links as semantic tokens (reusing text category tokens for flexibility and reusability) versus specific tokens (creating dedicated link namespaces for clarity and explicit purpose). The choice depends on system complexity, with semantic tokens being better suited for scalable systems that support multiple themes, while specific tokens work well for simpler systems with clearly differentiated link behaviors.

articles.title: LUCA BJØRNSTEN REVELS IN THE SUGARINESS OF POP CULTURE IN THESE LUMINOUS PAINTINGS
articles.summary: Luca Bjørnsten, aka Nugget Brain, creates vibrant, nostalgic art that blends vaporwave aesthetics with childhood ephemera, drawing from 90s pop culture, meme formats, and everyday American scenes like gas stations and supermarkets.

articles.title: WHY MENUS ARE DISAPPEARING IN MODERN UX
articles.summary: Traditional menus are disappearing from modern interfaces as designers shift from instruction-based to intuition-based UX that anticipates user needs.
